title: Evangelion on a major network
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion on a major network...             Quote Post by cd603 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 6:17 pm Ok, so assuming that Evangelion was originally aired on TV Tokyo in Japan, how do you guys think it would have been received by American audience if it was to be shown on a major network? (ie. CBS, FOX, etc.) I'm pretty sure that TV Tokyo is one of Japan's leading networks so majority of the general public there would have been able to watch it like a normal tv show. (I believe there were complaints about how children were able to watch it due to it's early evening time-slot) Here's my thoughts: ~I believe many American viewers (and Canadian viewers..lol) would have probably changed the channel considering it was animated and not live action ~The people who would stick by it would be drawn in by the violence and slowly would spread news of the the show by mouth to mouth conversation ~I think anyone who would have watched shows like Star Trek and some old 80's action movies would become interested... ~Naturally, a cult following would occur and the die hard fans would start purchasing merchandise by the boatload... ~When the ending would air, many would become enraged by the complete 360 degree flip of the series ~Angry fans would riot and eventually, due to the violence, a movie would be made that would have been all action and not confusing, psychological stuff that people couldn't get I don't know that's just my opinion about it...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Sep 02, 2010 6:21 pm Cartoon Network/[Adult Swim] isn't a large network?

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 6:29 pm Sachi wrote: Cartoon Network/[Adult Swim] isn't a large network?

[]: Re: Evangelion on a major network...             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Sep 02, 2010 7:24 pm cd603 wrote: Ok, so assuming that Evangelion was originally aired on TV Tokyo in Japan, how do you guys think it would have been received by American audience if it was to be shown on a major network? (ie. CBS, FOX, etc.) I'm pretty sure that TV Tokyo is one of Japan's leading networks so majority of the general public there would have been able to watch it like a normal tv show.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Thu Sep 02, 2010 7:26 pm When I think of Evangelion, I think of FOX. If the people are fox aren't trying to get the Eva license, they aren't doing thier Job, put it in the Animation domination block, and have the entire show redubed by the cast of Family Guy.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Thu Sep 02, 2010 7:43 pm That would be like the first Star Wars spoof and Something Something Something Darkside IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 8:34 pm If it were on a major network, probably CW11, the company would've have censored a lot of content. (Probably bigger/same than/as Cartoon Network Toonami's attempt in the frist 2 episodes). Also, for one thing, End of Eva would be 100% censored or banned. I'm just glad this never happened.

[]: Re: Evangelion on a major network...             Quote Post by cd603 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 10:24 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Incorrect. Evangelion was not initially aired to a wide audience. It was only broadcast to a few areas. It didn't gain mainstream popularity until it was rebroadcast later nationwide.

[]: Re: Evangelion on a major network...             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Sep 02, 2010 10:29 pm cd603 wrote: Ok but eventually it was on a tv channel that was accessible to people nationwide...

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Sep 02, 2010 10:32 pm These days ratings are everything to any broadcaster. The local station (ABS-CBN Channel 2) did aired EvaTV 10 years ago. The first run? Well, it dropped my jaw that they played the show damn straight -- Rei's nudity, the Reiquarium, the slaughter of Unit-03, etc. -- and so little cut down. Subsequent replays (they made three replays, if I remember correctly), however, were a pain, as they threw out the juicy parts (the local MTRCB and the big-mouthed conservatives went anal), and then turned EvaTV into a filler. Eventually the station let the contract run down and EvaTV was never aired again (due to lack of general viewer interest); the station (and its rivals) was more interested in LCD programming, where live-action soap operas are everything and these shows make more money (and the only anime that will make money for them are Naruto and Bleach and whatever that can be pulled out of Shonen Jump's ass). Now if the local HERO TV (it's a anime cable TV channel competing against AnimAXE SEA) had the balls to run Utena all the way, then why not they try EvaTV again?

[]: Re: Evangelion on a major network...             Quote Post by cd603 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 10:58 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: So is Cartoon Network. What's your point?

[]: Re: Evangelion on a major network...             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 11:10 pm cd603 wrote: Actually I guess I should have done a little research into Tv Tokyo...apparently they specialize in broadcasting anime (I thought they also had news, sports, etc.) So actually, yes cartoon network would be the correct choice because it's programming is animation as well, but i'm saying what would the public's reaction be if it appeared on a network where they broadcast shows like CSI and Law and Order? (Sorry i didn't know Tv Tokyo was just all anime..)

[]: Re: Evangelion on a major network...             Quote Post by cd603 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 11:18 pm Darkman.exe213 wrote: I would think it would get canceled due to low ratings. Shows like CSI and Law & Order might have decent writing and be able to appeal to intelligent people, but much of their audiences are still people who would rather turn off their brains when they're watching TV. Evangelion is way too different from those shows, and the typical audience of those shows still tend to automatically associate animation with little kids, unless it's some kind of adult swim comedy.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Sep 03, 2010 3:26 am NGETV used to run on VOX(German Major Channel) late at nights and the NGE fanbase in Germany and Austria is pretty big. Nothing outrageous happened. Cowboy Bepop used to run on the same timeslot too afaik

[]:             Quote Post by Zenithfleet » Mon Sep 06, 2010 7:39 am Almost interesting facts from down under ahoy! In Australia NGE was shown (dubbed) on a national network, SBS, around 1998-99. Admittedly it's the smallest of the five national channels, but still, it's free-to-air. And it's a 'serious' public broadcasting channel with a mandate to show multicultural content from all over the world. The only other anime on free TV back then was early-morning Pokemon, Sailor Moon and the like, so it made quite an impression. Wikipedia wrote: In 1998, Evangelion was the first anime series broadcast by Australia's SBS Television also airing in prime time. News of the broadcast slowly spread, and as a result, there was an upsurge of viewers midway through the season. This led to the unprecedented move of airing the series a second time from the beginning (SBS had acquired a licence to broadcast the series twice over), despite the fact the first airing had not yet completed. The success of Evangelion prompted SBS to gain the rights to several other anime series and the two Evangelion movies, which it later broadcast in their entirety.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Sep 06, 2010 4:10 pm if Evangelion was actually broadcast on a large U.S. broadcast network like CBS or FOX, I think it would have lasted all 26 episodes, but with a catch I think it would start out as easily the highest rated debut for an animated show and one of the highest rated show debuts of all time but, there would be controversy by the time Shinji spies TEH-REI-ZOMG!!!!! stark naked and feels Rei's glorious bewb in episode 5, and then it would be the highest rated show on TV because of this and other things, i.e. "thermal expansion," Toji's death, Yui-sama snarfs down Zeruel and paints the town red, implied Kaji-Misato bedroom fun, etc. and by the time Kaworu shows up, people will be polarized by the show if they have not been by then, with some saying it is the most evil thing on TV since Jersey Shore or that it is the greatest action drama in TV history and the finale would put The Sopranos finale to shame in notoriety

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Mon Sep 06, 2010 11:53 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: and the finale would put The Sopranos finale to shame in notoriety

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Sep 07, 2010 1:11 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: I think it would start out as easily the highest rated debut for an animated show and one of the highest rated show debuts of all time

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Wed Sep 08, 2010 11:02 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I'm sorry, you've exceeded the allotted amount of wishful thinking for your IP address today. Please upgrade to premium or wait 24 hours.
